transcript_events.text: The supervisor toolbar tab should have the input and send buttons docked at the bottom, with the content area scrolling. Also, I noticed that I cleared the log but I don't see the active session doing anything? Help me draft a goal to correct.
Sent 1 queued message to the provider.

I’ll trace the supervisor toolbar’s layout and session/log update path in this standalone worktree, prototype enough to identify the real failure mode, then turn that evidence into a concrete, ambitious goal with verifiable behavior.

The layout issue is already concrete: the supervisor wraps the entire shared chat panel inside an extra conversation container, but that wrapper has no flex sizing, so the composer participates in normal flow instead of remaining at the bottom. I’m now tracing “Clear history” against the live session/event cursor, because the second symptom may be more than a presentation bug.

The second symptom is architectural, not just stale pixels. “Clear history” currently calls the generic chat-clear path, which explicitly stops the supervisor session—even though the Supervisor capability’s own contract says there are no manual lifecycle controls. Meanwhile reconnect restoration is only a ten-event SSE replay and there is no explicit full-session hydration in the toolbar, so an existing session can appear active with an empty or partial transcript. I’m validating that gap with the focused tests and the documented capability contract now.

Focused tests are green, which is useful because it exposes the missing coverage: they only prove restoration from browser local storage, not from the authoritative chat record. I also checked the live durable state. The supposedly inactive session actually received the prompt at 10:56, streamed progress at 10:57, and completed at 10:58; the UI failed to make that work visible. The goal should therefore fix session continuity and observability, not merely add a spinner.

queued_messages.text: Also, the activity panel should be collapsed by default.
